explanation: The structured natural language property captures the requirement that when the system is operating in PCV or PSV mode, if the operator presses and holds the Inspiratory Pause button at the end of the inspiratory phase, then Inspiratory Pause (IP) will be initiated and both the inspiratory and expiratory valves will remain closed, but only for as long as the button is held (and not beyond 40 seconds). This is derived by constraining the property to be applicable only when in PCV or PSV mode and when the operator presses the inspiratory pause button at the end of the inspiratory phase. The closing of both valves is captured by 'inValveClose & outValveClose', and a duration constraint of maximum 40 seconds is indicated.

decision3_substring: as long as the operator holds the button but no longer than 40 sec

explanation: The structured natural language property captures the requirement that, in both PCV and PSV modes, it must be possible to initiate an inspiratory pause by pressing a dedicated button. This pause is enacted by closing both the inspiratory and expiratory valves at the end of the inspiratory phase and should be maintained as long as the operator holds the button, but not longer than 40 seconds. For this translation, 'PCVMode | PSVMode' indicates operation is in the required ventilation modes. The property applies whenever both modes are active. While the button ('inspiratoryPauseButton') is pressed at the end of the inspiratory phase ('inspiratoryPhaseEnd'), both valves ('inValveClose & outValveClose') must be closed. The temporal limit is captured as 'within 40 ticks'. The substring assignment maps natural language segments to corresponding decisions.

explanation: The original requirement states that, in PCV and PSV ventilation modes, a single button can be pressed to initiate an Inspiratory Pause (IP). When activated at the end of the inspiratory phase, both inspiratory and expiratory valves close and remain so as long as the operator holds the button, but never for more than 40 seconds. The structured NL property is formulated by specifying that in PCV or PSV mode, when the Inspiratory Pause Button is pressed at the end of the inspiratory phase, both the inspiratory and expiratory valves should close immediately and remain closed while the button is held, up to a 40-second maximum. Atomic propositions `PCVMode`, `PSVMode`, `inspiratoryPauseButton`, `inspiratoryPhaseEnd`, `inValveClose`, and `outValveClose` are used to match this intent (since these correspond to mode selection, pause button, phase end, and valve controls).

decision3_substring: as long as the operator holds the button but no longer than 40 sec

This template ensures that while in the correct modes and when the conditions apply (button press at the right moment and held), the two valves are closed for up to 40 seconds (N_DURATION=40), as required by the input sentence.

The atomic propositions are used as follows: PCVMode and PSVMode identify the modes, inspiratoryPauseButton identifies when the button is pressed, inValveClose and outValveClose for closing the valves, and inspiratoryPause for the pause state. explanation: This structured natural language property captures the requirement that when the ventilator is in either PCV or PSV mode, there must be a possibility for the operator to press a button that initiates an Inspiratory Pause (IP). During this pause, both inspiratory and expiratory valves are closed at the end of the inspiratory phase, and the pause is maintained as long as the button is continuously held, but not for longer than 40 seconds. The mapping uses the available atomic propositions as follows: 'PCVMode' (PCV mode active), 'PSVMode' (PSV mode active), 'inspiratoryPauseButton' (button is pressed/held), 'inspiratoryPause' (inspiratory pause is active), 'inValveClose' and 'outValveClose' (both valves closed), and 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd' (end of inspiratory phase). Since the atomic propositions do not provide a direct way to express 'as long as the operator holds the button, but no longer than 40 sec', the property defines that whenever both PCV or PSV mode are active and the button is pressed at end of inspiratory phase, then within 40 ticks (representing seconds), inspiratoryPause, inValveClose, and outValveClose are active. This captures the intention of closing valves for an inspiratory pause that is operator-initiated, time-limited, and mode-dependent.

decision3_substring: as long as the operator holds the button but no longer than 40 sec
output_structured_natural_language: while (PCVMode | PSVMode), whenever (inspiratoryPauseButton & inspiratoryPhaseEnd), within 40 ticks satisfy (inspiratoryPause & inValveClose & outValveClose)

explanation: The requirement states that, in either PCV or PSV modes, there must be the possibility for the operator to press and hold a single button to initiate an Inspiratory Pause (IP). This pause will close both inspiratory and expiratory valves at the end of the inspiratory phase, sustaining this closure for as long as the operator holds the button, but not exceeding 40 seconds. The atomic propositions 'PCVMode', 'PSVMode', 'user_equalTo_operator', 'inspiratoryPauseButton', 'inValveClose', and 'outValveClose' are used to model these facts. The logic is composed such that while in either ventilation mode and with the operator holding the button, at the end of the inspiratory phase, both valves close (inValveClose & outValveClose, which models the IP), and this is maintained up to 40 seconds or until the button is released.

decision3_substring: closes both inspiratory and expiratory valve at the end of the inspiratory phase as long as the operator holds the button but no longer than 40 sec

The closure is maintained as long as the button is held but not longer than 40 seconds.
